steps.title: Identify the center from the circle equation
steps.content: The equation $(x - a)^2 + (y - 17)^2 = r^2$ is in standard form. The center is $(a, 17)$.

steps.title: Use the midpoint formula with the diameter endpoints
steps.content:
The center of a circle is the midpoint of any diameter. With endpoints $(a, 11)$ and $(a, d)$, the midpoint is:

$$\left(a, \frac{11 + d}{2}\right)$$

Setting this equal to the center $(a, 17)$:

$$\frac{11 + d}{2} = 17$$

$$11 + d = 34$$

$$d = 23$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
